Metadata.siteDescription: AI-powered DOM inspection and context awareness. Chrome extension with MCP integration for Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, and more.

HomePage.metaDescription: AI-powered DOM inspection and context awareness for any MCP-compatible AI coding tool. Inspect elements, capture structured context, and feed it directly to your AI via MCP.
HomePage.heroSubtitle: DomLens bridges the gap between AI coding agents and the live DOM. Inspect elements, capture structured context, and feed it directly to your AI tools via MCP.
HomePage.installStep1Desc: Add DomLens to Chrome to inspect and select page elements.
HomePage.installStep2Desc: Send this to your AI tool to auto-configure the MCP connection.
HomePage.mcpSetupLink: View setup guide and supported tools →
HomePage.featuresTitle: Everything You Need for AI-Powered DOM Inspection
HomePage.featuresSubtitle: From element inspection to AI context delivery, DomLens covers the full workflow.
HomePage.howItWorksSubtitle: Get started in three simple steps
HomePage.step1Desc: Add DomLens to Chrome and start the local runtime server.
HomePage.step2Desc: Hover over any element to capture its structure, styles, and layout.
HomePage.step3Desc: Your AI tools automatically get structured DOM context via MCP.
HomePage.mcpSubtitle: Works with any MCP-compatible AI coding tool
HomePage.mcpDesc: DomLens runs a local MCP server that exposes 6 tools for AI agents. Configure once, and any MCP-compatible tool gains real-time DOM awareness.
HomePage.ctaTitle: Ready to Supercharge Your AI Workflow?
HomePage.ctaSubtitle: Install DomLens and give your AI coding agents the power of runtime DOM awareness.

inspector.title: Element Inspector
inspector.description: Hover over any DOM element to see detailed information including styles, attributes, computed properties, and box model visualization.

multiSelect.title: Multi-Element Selection
multiSelect.description: Select multiple elements simultaneously with Ctrl+Click. Each selection is labeled and can be managed independently.

frameworkAware.title: Framework Aware
frameworkAware.description: Automatically detects React and Vue components. Captures component names, props, and source locations for precise AI context.

mcp.title: MCP Integration
mcp.description: Native MCP server works with Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, VS Code + Cline, Warp, and any MCP-compatible AI tool.

compression.title: Context Compression
compression.description: Intelligent compression engine that reduces DOM context to the most relevant information for efficient AI consumption.

inlinePrompt.title: Inline Prompt
inlinePrompt.description: Write prompts directly on the page near selected elements. Describe what you want fixed and send it straight to your AI tool.

panel.title: Floating Panel
panel.description: Draggable inline panel showing selected element details. Send context directly to your AI tool from the panel.

livePatch.title: Live Patch
livePatch.description: AI modifies source code and signals the browser to refresh instantly. See changes in real-time without manual page reloads.

autoDiscovery.title: Auto-Discovery Server
autoDiscovery.description: Local runtime server with automatic port detection (4777-4787). No manual configuration needed.

getLatestContext.name: get_latest_context
getLatestContext.desc: Get the most recent DOM context captured by the inspector

getPrompt.name: get_prompt
getPrompt.desc: Get a formatted Markdown prompt ready to paste into AI chat

listContexts.name: list_contexts
listContexts.desc: List all stored DOM context history entries

clearContexts.name: clear_contexts
clearContexts.desc: Clear all stored DOM context history

getUserPrompt.name: get_user_prompt
getUserPrompt.desc: Get the latest user prompt submitted from the floating panel

applyRuntimePatch.name: apply_runtime_patch
applyRuntimePatch.desc: Signal the browser to refresh after AI modifies source code

ContextShowcase.title: What AI Actually Sees
ContextShowcase.subtitle: When you select an element, DomLens captures rich structured context and delivers it directly to your AI tool via MCP.
ContextShowcase.valueHtml: <button class="cta-primary px-6 py-3 rounded-lg font-semibold">Get Started</button>

Workflow.title: The Development Loop
Workflow.subtitle: A seamless cycle from inspection to implementation — AI sees, understands, fixes, and refreshes

select.title: Select Elements
select.desc: Alt+Click to inspect. Ctrl+Click for multi-select with labeled badges.

context.title: Capture Context
context.desc: Structured DOM data with styles, layout chain, and framework info.

ai.title: AI Understands
ai.desc: Your AI tool receives context via MCP and generates fixes.

patch.title: Apply Patch
patch.desc: AI modifies your source files directly with full context.

refresh.title: Live Refresh
refresh.desc: Browser updates instantly. See changes without manual reload.

HelpPage.metaDescription: Get help with DomLens. Find answers to frequently asked questions, usage guides, and keyboard shortcuts.
HelpPage.title: Help Center
HelpPage.subtitle: Everything you need to know about DomLens
HelpPage.guideSubtitle: Get started with DomLens in minutes
HelpPage.methodADesc: Send a single message to your AI tool — it will read the installation guide and configure everything automatically. No manual file editing required.
HelpPage.methodANote: Works with Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, and any MCP-compatible AI tool. The AI reads the guide, checks your Node.js version, writes the MCP config (preserving your existing settings), and verifies the installation — all automatically.
HelpPage.installStep1: Install the DomLens extension from the Chrome Web Store, or load it as an unpacked extension in developer mode.
HelpPage.installStep2: The runtime server starts automatically when configured via MCP. To run standalone:
HelpPage.installStep3: Add the following to your AI tool's MCP configuration file:
HelpPage.installStep4: Restart your AI tool to load the MCP config. Then open any webpage, Alt+Click an element, and check if your AI can call get_latest_context successfully.
HelpPage.compatibleDesc: DomLens works with any tool that supports the Model Context Protocol (MCP) via stdio transport. Same config, different config file locations:
HelpPage.shortcutsSubtitle: Quick reference for all available shortcuts

faq.a: DomLens is a Chrome extension that provides AI coding agents with runtime DOM context awareness. It allows AI tools like Claude Code, Cursor, and Windsurf to understand the live state of web page elements.

The easiest way is to send "安装 DomLens MCP: https://domlens.com/install/mcp-install.md" to your AI tool — it will configure everything automatically. Alternatively, add the MCP config manually to your tool's settings file.
Any tool that implements the Model Context Protocol (MCP). This includes Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, VS Code + Cline, Warp, and any other MCP-compatible client.
DomLens runs a local MCP server via stdio that exposes 6 tools. Your AI tool launches it via npx, and it automatically has access to DOM context data captured by the browser extension.
Yes. All DOM context data is processed locally on your machine. The MCP server communicates via stdio with your AI tool. No data is sent to external servers.
Alt+Hover to inspect, Click to select, Ctrl+Click for multi-select, Escape to close overlays. Shortcuts are fully customizable in the extension settings.
Hold Ctrl (or Cmd on Mac) and click on elements to select multiple items. Each selection is labeled A-J and shown in the floating panel. Press Escape to remove the last selection.

faq.q: Does it work with React and Vue?
faq.a: Yes. DomLens automatically detects React and Vue components, capturing component names, props, and source file locations for precise AI context.

faq.q: How does the live patch feature work?
faq.a: After AI modifies your source code, the apply_runtime_patch MCP tool signals the browser to refresh, letting you see changes instantly without manual page reloads.

faq.q: What is the natural language install method?
faq.a: Instead of manually editing config files, just send the installation guide URL to your AI tool. It reads the guide, checks your environment, writes the MCP config (preserving existing settings), and verifies everything works — all automatically.
